body:
closes https://github.com/CliMA/ClimaCalibrate.jl/issues/287, closes https://github.com/CliMA/ClimaCalibrate.jl/issues/283

This PR adds `SlurmConfig` and `PBSConfig` that will be part of the `HPCBackend` structs. Compared to the current implementation, the user can also specify the environment variables and modules which was a pain point for the calibration pipeline. Furthermore, there is more modularity in how the directives, modules, and environment variables are parsed, validated, and transformed to be used in the job scripts.

TODO

- [ ] ~~Add job scheduler agnostic config with mapping to `SlurmBackend` and `PBSBackend`~~
- [x] Add parsing for single character directives (e.g. `-b` instead of `--begin`)

I learned that the current job configuration for PBS assumes that you pass in directives that follows the Slurm naming conventions and when making the job script, it translates them to match the PBS naming conventions. Given this design, introducing a job scheduler agnostic config would add unnecessary complexity without benefit.
title: Add job scheduler configs for HPCBackends
